query: I want to evaluate one prompt across 50 multiple-choice scenarios. Grading is exact-match against the expected answer — no LLM judge needed. Set up the harness.
expected_behavior: Scaffolds the harness with judge_model set to null (or its equivalent) — does NOT force an LLM-judge for an exact-match grading task
Still emits the other four required fields per result row (model_id with revision pin, dataset_hash, prompt_version, result_row) even though judge_model is null
Uses or specifies programmatic / exact-match grading in place of an LLM judge (e.g., a grader function inside eval_runner.py or a regex-based check) for the multiple-choice answers
